title: trifolium pallescens var. arvernense
intro: Trifolium pallescens var. arvernense, also known as pale clover, is a variety of clover plant, a type of legume that belongs to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with medicinal properties attributed to their high levels of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Pale clover has been used to treat conditions such as fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, with its health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
